blocks.content: Leaving Reina's mansion, Shirone caught a carriage to the Magic Association.
With Rian dropping out and leaving on his knight's quest, the schedule had moved up sooner than expected.
Although he could have stayed at Reina's mansion for another day, he chose to head to the Association.
The spark Rian left behind ignited a fire in everyone's hearts.
Tess immediately returned home, saying she would start training right away, and Shirone also wanted to immerse himself in the atmosphere of the Magic Association, the pinnacle of magic.
Upon arriving at the Association building, Shirone asked for Plu at the reception desk.
After the receptionist relayed the message through an internal line, Plu came down five minutes later.
She frowned as soon as she saw Shirone.
With Kangnan also out, there was no one to keep her in check.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Why are you back so soon? Acting like you're so busy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: My business finished early. Can I stay here?
Plu's gaze towards Shirone was far from kind.
Without showing any sign of hesitation, he bluntly asked for a place to stay.
This was the prestigious Magic Association, not a hostel for kids with nowhere else to go.
However, since Kangnan had instructed her, she couldn't outright refuse.
With many eyes watching, Plu took Shirone to her designated area on the third floor.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: The Chief Secretary specifically asked me to look after you, but you have to follow my instructions. If you disobey or wander around, I'll kick you out. Got it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes.
From the start, Plu's attitude was prickly, leaving Shirone bewildered.
He hadn't done anything wrong, yet she had been unfriendly since their first meeting.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Here, you can stay here.
Plu offered her own quarters, which she used when working late.
The small room had bunk beds attached to both walls.
Shirone cautiously unpacked his things.
But before he could settle in, Plu called him out.
Follow me. I'll give you a tour.
Plu walked down the corridor with her magic staff held behind her back, her knees straight, and her chin tilted up at a 15-degree angle.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: First, let me introduce myself. I'm Ravid Plu, 22 years old. I graduated top of my class from the Royal Magic School and am currently a certified 8th-grade magician.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: I'll be a 6th-grade in two years. My current position is the manager of the third floor of the Magic Association's security department.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, I see. I'm...
As Shirone began to introduce himself, she stopped and turned coldly.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: I know. Arian Shirone. Attending Alpheas Magic School. Can use the archangel's power, Ataraxia, and specializes in light magic. Your unique Unlocker ability is mass attribution.
Shirone was stunned.
Someone who didn't even know his face the day before now knew everything about him.
The most puzzling thing was her overly prickly attitude towards him.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: You have a student named Dante at your school, right? He transferred recently.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. Do you know Dante?

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Why should I know Dante?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, I just thought since he also attended the Royal Magic School...
Plu's sharp gaze made Shirone shrink.
He couldn't figure out what to say to please her.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: When I was a senior, Dante was a junior. I know you beat Dante. But if you think that makes you special, you should stop. If I were three years younger, I'd be the top student in the kingdom, not you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I never acted special...
Shirone swallowed his words.
He felt that no matter what he said, he wouldn't get a good response.
As Plu patrolled the third floor, she occasionally introduced the departments.
Even the guards with graying hair bowed politely when they saw her.
Each time, she nodded briefly with a proud posture.
Plu was trying to show the successful side of a magician.
The tour was essentially a way to flaunt her position and train Shirone.
At that moment, a man drenched in sweat and carrying documents walked down the corridor.
Plu's confident face momentarily showed tension.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Hello, Mr. Sakiri. Good morning.
Shirone looked closely at the man called Sakiri.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So that's Sakiri.
After the Arcane incident, when Alpheas and Kanis were summoned to the Association, the investigator in charge was named Sakiri.
He looked like a tired old man, but his eyes were sharper than anyone Shirone had ever met.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Hello. Who's this? A new face.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Oh, just giving a tour. Part of a special activity.
The Magic Association, being the core institution of the kingdom's magical society, received numerous tour requests from students.
So Sakiri just nodded and walked away.
Plu let out a small sigh and looked at Shirone.
The atmosphere, which had been stiff with her head held high, now felt awkward.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: That was Mr. Sakiri, a certified 5th-grade magician. He's very famous in the inspection department. Any criminal caught by him won't even have bones left.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, I've heard of him.
For the first time, Plu smiled with satisfaction.
Knowing that even a mere magic school student was aware of someone she worked with restored some of her pride.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now I have a rough idea of how to handle him.
Thinking this, Shirone licked his lips when a man got off the elevator at the end of the corridor.
Seeing Plu, he approached, his eyes lighting up with interest when he saw Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Plu, is that Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Oh, Mr. Gando. Hello.
The man's name was Klein Gando, a member of Gaold's elite guard like Plu.
Known as 'Gando of Thunder,' he was a combat magician skilled in electric magic, holding the position of head of security at the Magic Association and a certified 4th-grade magician.
He was tall, wore tight leather pants and studded boots, and had purple hair slicked back with one strand left out.
Despite his flashy appearance, his face looked angelic.
Plu led Shirone to greet Gando.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: I see. Nice to meet you. I'm Gando.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, hello.
As Shirone was about to bow, he quickly shook Gando's hand.
The awkward combination of a bow and handshake made Gando laugh.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: You look much younger than I expected. I thought you'd have a scary face.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is that my image?
Having rarely left Tormia, Shirone couldn't know.
But recalling the articles in the journals, he thought it was possible to be misunderstood.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Are you on a regular patrol?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. Starting from the top floor. How's the third floor? Any issues?

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: No, as usual. Hohoho!
Plu laughed, covering her mouth with pride as the third-floor manager.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Good. It seems Ms. Kangnan was summoned by the Chairman. She should be back with him tomorrow. Just relax until then.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you.
Gando's kind demeanor made Shirone want to complain, 'This sister is bullying me,' but he knew it would only cause trouble, so he kept it to himself.
As Gando headed back to the elevator, Plu's smiling face turned stern.
She resumed scolding Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: He's in charge of security at the Magic Association. My direct superior. You've heard of Gando of Thunder, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Since Shirone knew about Sakiri, she assumed he would know Gando too.
Yes, I've heard of him.
In truth, Shirone had never heard the name before.
But in this situation, it was best to pretend he knew.
Plu was satisfied with Shirone's compliant attitude.
Feeling that the scolding was enough, she brought up what had been on her mind.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Do you know why the Chairman wants to see you? It's related to Heaven, right?
Shirone remained silent, his expression serious.
Even if Plu was someone Kangnan trusted, he had to maintain security.
Seeing his sharp gaze, Plu realized she couldn't treat him lightly.
She felt both impressed and suspicious, wondering if his previous demeanor had been an act.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Anyway, it's my job to assist the Chairman. I'll definitely go to Heaven. So don't get cocky just because you got invited once.
Shirone had never been cocky, nor did he intend to be.
But her words gave him a clue about her hostility.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You must really respect the Chairman.
Plu's expression flickered like a fish out of water.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Of course! Mr. Sakiri and Mr. Gando are impressive, but they can't compare to Mikael Gaold.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's true. He's a certified 1st-class Archmage.
Hohoho!
Plu laughed condescendingly, making Shirone's blood boil.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: You really don't know anything. Listen carefully. There are four 1st-grade class mages in the Tormia Kingdom.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: But being the Chairman of the Magic Association isn't just about being strong. It requires political skill and resourcefulness.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: So why was Mr. Gaold chosen as Chairman?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is he highly skilled in many areas?
Shirone thought about it but couldn't grasp the level of a 1st-grade Grand Magician, so he couldn't answer.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: No, it's the opposite. He's not just strong; he's overwhelmingly strong.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Overwhelmingly strong?
Right.
He can maintain his position as Chairman despite being labeled a madman because of his overwhelming strength, which sets him apart from other 1st-grade Grand Magicians.
He's the strongest! To a combat magician like me, he's an idol and an ideal. How could I not respect him?
Hearing Plu's words, Shirone realized that the Chairman was an even more extraordinary person than he had imagined.
This made him even more curious about why such a person wanted to see him.
Plu turned away without hesitation and said, 'Anyway, I have to get back to work, so stay in your room until dinner. The tour is over, so don't wander around without my permission.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah! Um...
As Shirone called out urgently, Plu turned back.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Before you go... can I try the elevator just once?
Plu looked at Shirone and smiled, but then her face turned cold again, and she replied curtly, 'No.'
Shirone watched her walk away, feeling bewildered.
What did I do wrong?
After spending a day that felt as monotonous as prison life, the next morning arrived.
Shirone decided to sleep in since he couldn't leave the room until Plu arrived.
As he tried to get more sleep, he heard bustling noises outside.
In a daze, he saw Plu burst into the room and shout, 'Hey, rookie! Are you still sleeping?'

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Are you still sleeping?
Shirone groggily opened his eyes and sat up.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, good morni...
Do you think this is your bedroom at home? Get up quickly!

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Do you think this is your bedroom at home? Get up quickly!
Now Shirone was starting to get annoyed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Um, is there something I need to do?
Did you think you could eat and sleep for free? Today, you'll follow me around and assist.
I'll give you 10 minutes, so wash up and get ready!
Shirone felt like he had been hit by a bomb while sleeping.
Struggling to wake up, he slowly leaned back and fell onto the bed again.
But he couldn't stay there for more than 10 seconds before he curled up and got out of bed.
Ugh, this is too much.
